name: Iteratron

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + at least 3 unlocked/visible layers, with the same animatable property type present on all of them; user must select a property on the start layer and a property on the end layer via the palette buttons before clicking OK
consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem (active composition), curComp.selectedLayers[0].index and curComp.selectedProperties to capture start and end layer indices and property paths. On OK, resolves those property paths on every valid (unlocked + enabled) layer between the two indices and reads their current values plus numKeys to determine the write method.

controls.name: Link Start Property
controls.role: captures the currently selected layer index and property path as the interpolation start point; button label changes from ⚪️ to 🟢 on success

controls.name: Link End Property
controls.role: captures the currently selected layer index and property path as the interpolation end point; button label changes from ⚪️ to 🟢 on success

controls.name: OK
controls.role: executes interpolation — distributes property values linearly across all valid intermediate layers and closes the palette

controls.name: Cancel
controls.role: dismisses the palette without making any changes

functions: open a non-modal palette for linking start and end layer properties
capture layer index and property path from the current host selection
validate that start and end layers are unlocked and visible
count valid (unlocked + enabled) intermediate layers between the two linked indices
compute a linear step value for each dimension of the selected property
distribute interpolated property values across all valid intermediate layers
write values via setValue for static properties or setValueAtTime(currentTime) for keyframed properties
wrap all writes in a single undo group named 'Link Properties'
alert the user on invalid property selection, locked/hidden boundary layers, or zero valid intermediate layers

scriptSim.scenario: success – 2D Position distributed setValue across 2 intermediate layers
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Motion Comp' (1920×1080, 24 fps, 5 s) with 4 AV layers: Ball A (index 1, Position [0,540]), Ball B (index 2, Position [960,540]), Ball C (index 3, Position [960,540]), Ball D (index 4, Position [1920,540]). Start linked to Ball A / Transform / Position; end re-linked to Ball D / Transform / Position. No keyframes on any layer.

notes: The @ui frontmatter declares DIALOG but the script creates Window('palette', ...) — it behaves as a floating, non-modal palette that stays open until OK or Cancel is clicked.
Property paths are constructed using matchName when available, falling back to name; they are joined by commas (e.g. 'ADBE Transform Group,ADBE Position').
Multi-dimensional properties (2D Position, 3D Position, Color) are processed component-wise; each dimension gets its own step value.
Layer ordering is direction-agnostic: the script uses Math.min/Math.max of the two linked indices, so start can be above or below end in the layer stack.
Locked or hidden (enabled=false) layers between start and end are silently skipped and not counted in the interpolation step calculation.
If the target property has numKeys > 0, the script calls setValueAtTime(curComp.time, newValue) instead of setValue(newValue).
The script does not write any AE expressions; all values are baked at run time.
